segments.text: For example, AI has taught you that you have to make a mug and put the feature of AI in it.
You have learnt all AI and made an AI-driven coffee mug.
Then you came to know that the market where I am sitting, there is no buying a 5,000 mug.
The mug is fine, the feature is fine, the branding is fine, the promotion is fine, the technology is fine.
But you have not done business model feasibility analysis for two years.
And people are going to this extent that what is someone selling, what is someone selling, what are they doing?
Oh brother, first of all, do a business model analysis.
Then I gave it to a kid. He has been working for two years.
He took money from a non-technical investor.
He made a product that is GPO intensive and he does not know about the availability of GPO and the cost.
Brother, I asked him the same thing that when it becomes, you fly it to a 1 km drone.
If it takes a 1 km footage, there will be thousands of frames inside it.
When the model train is processed by those thousands of frames, then where will the GPO come from?
I said, sir, what is GPO? What about that kid? What is that idea?
And here we know why.
Because here, at any stage or at any video, someone motivates you so much that we forget this analysis.
We forget to criticize.
So for God's sake, if you're going to develop a product, develop a business, do the research,
critical analysis and go to a subject matter expert, maybe in a right industry, ask them, what is your problem?
And when you will be pushed again and again, then you will have an intellectual sense about that one particular idea.
And if that idea fails, then trust me, you will not fail.
Because you make it better than that.
You will solve the problem of that field.

summary.summary:
User: 
You are an AI assistant that understands videos.

Below is the transcript of the video.

Transcript
----------
For example, AI has taught you that you have to make a mug and put the feature of AI in it. You have learnt all AI and made an AI-driven coffee mug. Then you came to know that the market where I am sitting, there is no buying a 5,000 mug. The mug is fine, the feature is fine, the branding is fine, the promotion is fine, the technology is fine. But you have not done business model feasibility analysis for two years. And people are going to this extent that what is someone selling, what is someone selling, what are they doing? Oh brother, first of all, do a business model analysis. Then I gave it to a kid. He has been working for two years. He took money from a non-technical investor. He made a product that is GPO intensive and he does not know about the availability of GPO and the cost. Brother, I asked him the same thing that when it becomes, you fly it to a 1 km drone. If it takes a 1 km footage, there will be thousands of frames inside it. When the model train is processed by those thousands of frames, then where will the GPO come from? I said, sir, what is GPO? What about that kid? What is that idea? And here we know why. Because here, at any stage or at any video, someone motivates you so much that we forget this analysis. We forget to criticize. So for God's sake, if you're going to develop a product, develop a business, do the research, critical analysis and go to a subject matter expert, maybe in a right industry, ask them, what is your problem? And when you will be pushed again and again, then you will have an intellectual sense about that one particular idea. And if that idea fails, then trust me, you will not fail. Because you make it better than that. You will solve the problem of that field.

Visual Scene Information
------------------------
Scene 1
Timestamp: 12.33 seconds
Description: A man is standing at a podium with a blue logo on it. He is wearing a suit and tie.

Scene 2
Timestamp: 26.86 seconds
Description: A group of people are sitting in a lecture hall.

Scene 3
Timestamp: 37.30 seconds
Description: A man is standing at a podium with a blue plaque on it. He is wearing a suit and tie.

Scene 4
Timestamp: 46.60 seconds
Description: A group of people are sitting in a theater with their heads bowed.

Scene 5
Timestamp: 55.64 seconds
Description: A man is standing at a podium with a blue emblem on it. He is wearing a suit and tie.

Scene 6
Timestamp: 64.91 seconds
Description: A group of people are sitting in a lecture hall. The seats are teal and the walls are white.

Scene 7
Timestamp: 76.88 seconds
Description: A man is standing at a podium with a blue emblem on it. He is wearing a suit and tie.

Scene 8
Timestamp: 89.02 seconds
Description: A group of people are sitting in a lecture hall. The people are dressed in various colors, including pink, green, and purple. There are chairs and tables in the room.

Scene 9
Timestamp: 97.10 seconds
Description: A man is standing at a podium with a blue plaque on it. He is wearing a suit and tie.

Scene 10
Timestamp: 103.94 seconds
Description: The image is completely black. There are no objects, people, actions, or visible text present in the image.



Task
----

You are an expert at understanding and summarizing educational videos.

You are given two inputs:

1. Transcript
   - This is the primary source of information.
   - It contains the spoken content of the video.

2. Scene Descriptions
   - These describe the visual content of keyframes.
   - Use them only to provide visual context when they add useful information.

Instructions

1. Use the transcript as the primary source of information.
2. Use scene descriptions only when they improve the understanding of the content.
3. Focus on what the speaker is explaining, teaching, or discussing.
4. Do not repeatedly describe the speaker, audience, podium, or camera angle unless it is important to the content.
5. Generate a concise but informative summary.
6. Divide the video into meaningful chapters based on topic changes, not simply scene changes.
7. Each chapter should have:
   - title
   - start_time (in seconds)
   - short description

Return ONLY valid JSON in the following format:

{
    "summary": "...",
    "chapters": [
        {
            "title": "...",
            "start_time": 0.0,
            "description": "..."
        }
    ]
}

Assistant: {
    "summary": "The video discusses the importance of conducting a business model feasibility analysis before launching a product. It highlights the need for critical thinking and research to understand the market, target audience, and potential challenges. The speaker emphasizes the value of seeking advice from subject matter experts and avoiding the temptation to rush into a product without thorough analysis. The video also touches on the role of technology in product development and the potential for failure if not properly planned.",
    "chapters": [
        {
            "title": "Importance of Business Model Feasibility Analysis",
            "start_time": 0.0,
            "description": "The speaker stresses the need for a thorough business model feasibility analysis before launching a product. This includes understanding the market, target audience, and potential challenges. The speaker emphasizes the value of seeking advice from subject matter experts and avoiding the temptation to rush into a product without proper planning."
        },
        {
            "title": "Role of Technology in Product Development",
            "start_time": 26.86,
            "description": "The speaker discusses the role of technology in product development. He mentions that technology can be a significant factor in product development, but it is important to consider the potential challenges and limitations of technology in the context of the product being developed."
        },
        {
            "title": "Avoiding the Temptation to Rush into a Product",
            "start_time": 46.60,
            "description": "The speaker advises against rushing into a product without proper planning and analysis. He emphasizes the importance of taking the time to understand the market, target audience, and potential challenges before launching a product."
        }
    ]
}
